regimeSub: Sticky 4.2% CPI keeps Fed hawkish even as Iran war ends and oil cracks below $80, easing the supply-shock tail.
themes: Iran-US framework ends war -> Iranian barrels return, Brent sub-$80, supply premium bleeds out fast
CPI at 3-yr high 4.2% -> Fed drops cutting bias, more officials flag hikes; September live
Oil relief + AI mega-cap bid -> SPX 7500 risk-on despite no rate cuts coming
Trump's Fed pick can't deliver cuts into hot inflation -> credibility risk, term-premium creep in 10s

signalNotes.name: US Real Rate
signalNotes.note: Policy 3.63% vs CPI 4.2% = negative real front end; Fed boxed in, can't ease into hot prints.

signalNotes.name: 2s10s Curve
signalNotes.note: +29bp, modestly steep; market pricing higher-for-longer plus term premium, not recession cuts.

signalNotes.name: HY Credit Spread
signalNotes.note: Implied tight with VIX 16 and SPX at highs; credit ignoring hike risk, complacent.

signalNotes.name: VIX
signalNotes.note: 16.4 — war-end relief crushed vol; cheap hedges ahead of a hawkish Fed and CPI risk.

signalNotes.name: Brent Crude
signalNotes.note: $79.57, 3-month low; Iran ceasefire + tanker resumption removes geopolitical premium.

signalNotes.name: Gold
signalNotes.note: $4160 elevated; holding bid on negative real rates and Fed-credibility doubts, not war fear.

signalNotes.name: USD / KRW
signalNotes.note: 1538 weak won despite DXY 100.97; KOSPI 9033 melt-up masks FX-funding and export stress.

signalNotes.name: Semiconductor SOX
signalNotes.note: AI leadership intact, Nikkei/KOSPI parabolic; Accenture IT drag a minor wobble, not a turn.

headline: Iran war ends and oil breaks below $80, but US inflation at a 3-year high keeps a hawkish Fed on hold with hikes back on the table as risk assets melt up.
